analysis.headline: 12 learning candidates analyzed since 2026-01-01; 0 risky repositories excluded; top signal is topic:ai-agents.

repo_lessons.adoption_hypothesis: affaan-m/ECC is likely gaining attention because it combines AI/agent positioning, JavaScript ecosystem fit, clear topic packaging.
emulate: Use a concrete one-sentence value proposition.
Keep license and reuse rights explicit.
Show active maintenance through recent commits.
avoid: Avoid copying surface topics without reproducing the proof path.

reasons: Topic clustering is strong around ai-agents, claude-code, llm, which makes the value proposition easy to search and share.
JavaScript leads the language mix, so examples and packaging should respect that ecosystem.
AI and agent language is still a strong discovery hook when paired with a concrete workflow.
Developer automation projects benefit from short setup, visible output, and repeatable commands.

backlog.title: Ship a one-command demo loop
backlog.rationale: topic:ai-agents (4) is the strongest analysis signal, so the first success path should make that value obvious.
backlog.verification: A fresh clone can run the demo command and produce a report without secrets.

backlog.title: Publish daily trend evidence
backlog.rationale: The roadmap should show how current topic:ai-agents (4) evidence changes recommendations over time.
backlog.verification: A scheduled run updates a snapshot, star history, and daily brief.

backlog.title: Keep credentials optional and documented
backlog.rationale: Public GitHub data can be read unauthenticated, but tokens raise rate limits and enable repo automation.
backlog.verification: .env.local is ignored, `.env.example` is safe, and CI uses scoped tokens.

backlog.title: Expose the agent decisions as machine-readable JSON
backlog.rationale: Agent-oriented projects spread faster when downstream tools can reuse their decisions.
backlog.verification: The daily run writes both Markdown and JSON decision artifacts.

backlog.title: Tighten the CLI around repeatable maintainer workflows
backlog.rationale: Developer-tool projects win when routine jobs become short, memorable commands.
backlog.verification: Collect, monitor, analyze, and orchestrate commands have examples and tests.

backlog.title: Add a local insight view for trend comparisons
backlog.rationale: Data and UI signals point to users wanting inspectable local output, not only a generated brief.
backlog.verification: The report data can be opened locally and compared across at least two snapshots.

backlog.title: Create a public progress loop for Xiao-rx/agent-workbench
backlog.rationale: The target repository should show how trend data directly affects roadmap choices.
backlog.verification: The daily brief includes a target repo star sample and next action.

review_findings.title: Backlog has testable gates
review_findings.detail: Every proposed item includes a verification statement and can be reviewed before release.

git_observation.recommendation: Commit small, explainable improvements and watch the next star sample for response.
git_observation.next_sample_gate: After publishing Update daily trend brief, compare the next star sample with 0 stars and local delta 0.
